title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at 10% with no tradeable spot price yet. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus $3.00 spot median, a 134% dislocation reflecting 121.7% annualized volatility as training demand whipsaws a 45%-available tier.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at 10% with no tradeable spot price yet. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus $3.00 spot median, a 134% dislocation reflecting 121.7% annualized volatility as training demand whipsaws a 45%-available tier.

Applying 1.25x TCO to spot inference costs, Llama 70B on H100 runs $9.84/M tokens and on MI300X runs $11.90/M. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Qwen3-32B at $0.30/M are selling at catastrophic losses exceeding $9/M tokens on H100, burning cash to buy share. OpenAI grades Neutral with o3 Pro commanding premium at $80/M output; Anthropic grades Strong with Opus 4.6 holding $25/M; Google grades Weak at $2.50 Flash output; DeepSeek grades Burning Cash.

B200 at 25% availability and $4.20 spot is undervalued given utilization surging 5.6pp to 85.5% in 30 days, positioning for a squeeze as training clusters absorb remaining capacity. Frontier token pricing from OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued by roughly 3x versus Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M on comparable capability, making Opus the better risk-adjusted API allocation.
bullets: 25% availability on B200 puts next-gen compute squarely in the scarcity zone with spot ranging $3.50 to $5.50 per hour.
10% availability on GB200 signals severe shortage with no executable spot price published.
21.76 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch at base 100.
$0.06 per million output tokens on open-source Gemma-2-2b represents a 1,333x discount to o3 Pro at $80.
90.3% H100 utilization is up 10.5 percentage points in 30 days, the largest demand surge across all GPU classes.
$41.17 per DDR5 16Gb chip rose 1.15% week-over-week, the only memory component showing upward price pressure.
